prompt: A detailed 19th-century naturalist illustration of a sloping landscape resembling a botanical specimen, drawn in the style of scientific field studies from the 1800s. Small insects (such as ants or beetles) are shown at different positions along a curved terrain, gradually descending toward a central low point where many insects have gathered. The terrain resembles a natural surface like a leaf or eroded earth, subtly shaped like a basin. Fine ink linework, cross-hatching, delicate shading, muted sepia and natural tones, aged paper texture. No text, no labels, no modern elements. Composition resembles a scientific plate, with careful observational detail and balanced layout. Mood: analytical, calm, observational, precise.
tradition_approach: Used fine ink linework, cross-hatching, muted tones, and scientific plate composition to mimic naturalist illustration while mapping descent behavior through insects moving toward a basin-like minimum.
